sections.content:
SPACE didn't change anything visible. The board looks the same. Let me check what changed between step 200 and 201.
Let me look at the current state more carefully. The black F is merged with the yellow F. I need to figure out what the goal is. 
Let me think about this differently. The game has three F-shapes:
1. Charcoal F (left)
2. Black F with white dots (player, middle)  
3. Yellow F (right)
I moved the black F right 3 times and it overlapped the yellow F. Now the yellow F's top bar is visible but the rest seems merged.
Maybe I need to move the black F to overlap ALL the F shapes? Or maybe I need to move them into a specific configuration?
Let me try moving LEFT to separate from the yellow F and see what happens.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: SPACE didn't change the board. Let me check what happened and try moving LEFT to separate from the yellow F.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['pixels'] > 10:
        b = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
        max_r = max(p[0] for p in b)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
        max_c = max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"Color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min_r}-{max_r}, cols={min_c}-{max_c}, children={n['children']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
